subject: Split card with two scenes: left (Fuss) shows a crime-scene tableau of robed silhouetted witnesses standing over a fallen body and scattered blades in red firelight; right (Bother) shows a bald robed mage with outstretched arms conjuring spinning bladed artifact constructs amid magical sigils in cool blue light
description: A split/aftermath card with two stacked panels. The lower panel (Fuss) is rendered in fiery red and amber tones: dark cloaked figures stand in silhouette against a hazy red backdrop, looming over a body sprawled in the foreground beside scattered swords and weapons. Stark light streaks slash across the composition, suggesting a moment frozen mid-violence or its immediate aftermath. The upper panel (Bother) is rendered in cool teal and blue: a bald, dark-skinned robed figure with arms thrown wide stands at center, surrounded by three or more rotating bladed constructs shaped like geometric stars or sigil-discs. Faintly etched glyphs and runic patterns fill the background, and ghostly diagrams float across the field. The two halves contrast murder-mystery noir below with arcane summoning above, fitting the Murders at Karlov Manor detective-noir theme.
foreground: fallen body and scattered swords on Fuss side; mage and spinning bladed constructs on Bother side
background: robed silhouetted witnesses against red haze (Fuss); glyph-etched blue field with magical sigils (Bother)
emotion: Fuss reads as tense aftermath, witnesses frozen over a corpse; Bother reads as ecstatic invocation, the mage caught mid-conjuration with arms thrown wide
